title: basilima alpina
intro: Basilima alpina, a flowering plant native to the Andean region of South America, is a member of the Gentianaceae family and is also known as Andes gentian or mountain gentian. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Basilima alpina include its ability to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the plant are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
